other_observers: Brandon Percival and I first heard the bird singing. We debated as to wether it was a Bay-breasted or Cape May Warbler.; Brandon and I heard it, and Brandon first spotted it.  Later seen by Mark Peterson in the P.M. in the same tree where Brandon and I first saw it foraging.
specific_location: Between Homes ranch house and banding table area on The Chico Basin Ranch.
description: Nice bright male with yellow breast thinly streaked with black vertical lines. The yellow face had a chestnut auricular patch, a yellow supercillium, and a thin black eyeline. The bill was very thin and dark. The crown was dark, the back was dark greenish with faint dark streaks. It had yellowish uppertail and undertail coverts. I forgot to look at the leg color as I was way too excited and tried, without success to digiscope the bird.Behaviors: Forage slowly in lower level of Siberian elm while singing. Moved about and eventualy moved higher. After it stopped singing, I couldn't refind it. It moved about 150 feet distance in about 10 minutes.Call: yes, it sang a very high-pitched, thin, upslurred notes of short duration. The song is how we located the bird.Plumage: breeding
